items.name: awesome-openclaw-skills
items.summary: Large OpenClaw skill collection sourced from registry
items.install_cmd: OpenClaw is a locally-running AI assistant that operates directly on your machine. Skills extend its capabilities, allowing it to interact with external services, automate workflows, and perform specialized tasks. This collection helps you discover and install the right skills for your needs. It can also serve as a source of inspiration for OpenClaw use cases.
items.use_case: Find and compare skills quickly before deciding what to install or audit.
items.risk_explanation: Medium risk: likely includes executable workflow logic, automation, or external tool interaction; review before broad promotion.
items.why_include_now: Strong ecosystem signal, clear skill identity, and good value as an index anchor for the first Skill Tree library.
items.direct_value_to_skill_tree: Improves trust and coverage of a major category users will search for on day one.

safety_precheck.notes: Contains executable skill logic or automation; needs repo-level audit before inclusion.

items.name: awesome-claude-code
items.summary: A curated list of awesome skills, hooks, slash-commands, agent orchestrators, applications, and plugins for Claude Code by Anthropic
items.use_case: Use when one agent is not enough and you need orchestrated, multi-step, or delegated execution.
items.risk_explanation: Medium risk: likely involves executable automation, external tools, or broader workflow authority; review setup and permissions first.
items.install_friction: needs credentials/auth setup; requires Node/npm tooling; may require Python environment; may require MCP/server config; may require Docker or local services; review permissions before broad use
items.governance_rationale: repository signals support for scheduled/background or long-running workflows; repository mentions multi-agent, delegation, or orchestration patterns; repository references governance, security, permissions, or audit controls; protocol/integration hints found: MCP, CLI
items.why_include_now: Strong ecosystem signal, clear skill identity, and good value as an index anchor for the first Skill Tree library.
items.direct_value_to_skill_tree: Improves trust and coverage of a major category users will search for on day one.

safety_precheck.notes: Mostly registry/spec/content or local workflow scope; low obvious abuse surface from description.
